maml:description.maml:para: Get a content protection JavaScript injection rule.

List all JavaScript injection rules, or specify an individual by its `JavaScriptInjectionRuleID`. When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
ID of the javascript injection rule.
Your security policy's ID.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
The name of your security policy.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
ID of the javascript injection rule.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
The name of your security policy.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
ID of the javascript injection rule.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
ID of the javascript injection rule.
Your security policy's ID.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
The name of your security configuration.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
ID of the javascript injection rule.
Your security policy's ID.
The name of your security policy.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
Get a content protection rule.

List all content protection rules, or specify an individual by its `ContentProtectionRuleID`. When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Identifies a content protection rule.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your security policy's ID.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
The name of your security policy.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Identifies a content protection rule.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
The name of your security policy.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Identifies a content protection rule.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Identifies a content protection rule.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your security policy's ID.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
The name of your security configuration.
Identifies a content protection rule.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your security policy's ID.
The name of your security policy.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
Get the content protection rule sequence.

Get the sequence of your content protection rules. The rules are evaluated in the listed order. When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your security policy's ID.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
The name of your security policy.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
The name of your security policy.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your security policy's ID.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
The name of your security configuration.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your security policy's ID.
The name of your security policy.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
Create a content protection JavaScript injection rule.

Create a new content protection JavaScript injection rule. When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your security policy's ID.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
The name of your security policy.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
The name of your security policy.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your security policy's ID.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
Delete a content protection JavaScript injection rule.

Delete the specified JavaScript injection rule. When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.

Update the content protection rule sequence.

Modify the sequence of your content protection rules. The rules are evaluated in the listed order. When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.

Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your security policy's ID.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
The name of your security policy.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
The name of your security policy.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your security policy's ID.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your configuration's ID.
The name of your security configuration.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your security policy's ID.
The name of your security policy.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
A configuration's version. Can be an integer or the word `latest`.
